title: Junior Full Stack Engineer
description: Job Description: EXL (NASDAQ: EXLS) is a global data and artificial intelligence ("AI") company that offers services and solutions to reinvent client business models, drive better outcomes and unlock growth with speed. EXL harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world’s leading corporations in industries including insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media and retail, among others. EXL was founded in 1999 with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity and respect. We are headquartered in New York and have more than 60,000 employees spanning six continents. For more information, visit www.exlservice.com . **Role Title:** Full Stack Engineer (Junior level) **BU/Segment:** Digital **Location:** Dublin, Republic of Ireland (Flexible hybrid working) **Employment Type:** Permanent **As part of your duties, you will be responsible for:** * Design, develop, and maintain full\-stack applications using Python (backend) and React.js (frontend) * Develop APIs and microservices to support AI agent communications and data exchange * Implement real\-time features for agent monitoring, logging, and performance tracking * Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues across the full stack * Work closely with product managers, designers, and other engineers to deliver high\-quality solutions * Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code that prioritizes security and data protection. * Maintain high software quality standards within the team by adhering to best practices. Identify and encourage areas for growth and improvement within the group. * Work in an agile delivery/ DevOps methodology to deliver enterprise\-grade production implementation in iterative sprints. * Explore and leverage AI tools to help improve innovation, efficiency and quality across the SDLC. **Qualifications and experience we consider to be essential for the role:** * Minimum 1\+ Years of experience in application development \& support. * Experience in any programming language. * Experience with any frontend technology like react.js etc. * Understanding of any of the cloud platforms (AWS/Azure /GCP), * Knowledge of algorithms, design patterns, and fundamental computer science concepts \& data structures * Experience with CI/CD pipelines **Good to have (optional)** * Experience with AI/ML libraries such as LangChain, OpenAI API, Bedrock, or similar framework * Understanding of containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and infrastructure\-as\-code (Terraform, CDK). **Skills and Personal attributes we would like to have:** * Strong communication skills and ability to work with various stakeholders across various geographies * Master's or bachelor's degree, preferably from an Engineering Background * Be a self\-motivated, curious, and productive under high pressure **As part of a leading global Data and AI company, you can look forward to:** * A competitive salary with a generous bonus, private healthcare, life assurance at 4 x your annual salary, income protection insurance, and a rewarding pension. * At **EXL** , we are committed to providing our employees with the tools and resources they need to succeed and excel in their careers. We offer a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities. We also support a range of learning initiatives that allow our employees to build on their existing skills and knowledge. From online courses to seminars and workshops, our employees have the opportunity to enhance their skills and stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies. * As an Equal Opportunity Employer, **EXL** is committed to diversity. Our company does not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, age, or disability status. * At **EXL** , we offer a flexible hybrid working model that allows employees to live a balanced, healthy lifestyle while strengthening our culture of collaboration. **To be considered for this role, you must already be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland.**

title: Junior Fund Accountant
description: **About Us – Opening Up Wealth Together.** FNZ Group believes everyone can create a better life by saving, investing, and managing their money well. We know the foundation to do that already exists in the wealth management industry, but complexity holds firms back. We created wealth’s growth platform to help. We partner with over 650 financial institutions and 12,000 wealth managers, with US$1\.5 trillion in assets under administration (AUA). Together with our customers, we help over 24 million people from all wealth segments to invest in their future. **The Role** Junior Fund Accountant (14\-Month Fixed Term) Joining a team ranging in experience from recent graduates, through to colleagues who have progressed internally to professional qualification, this is an excellent career development opportunity. **The Key Responsibilities Of The Department Include** * Providing a high standard Fund Accounting service to FNZ Ireland’s clients * Valuing the funds in an accurate and timely manner and publication of Net Asset Values (NAVs) * Placing trades with international fund managers in accordance with the advice of the clients’ asset allocation advisers * Purchasing and selling foreign currencies in a manner approved by the client. * Treasury management of cash holdings * Recording all fund transactions in an accurate and timely manner * Preparation of all operational team output for capture on our imaging system * Producing monthly accounts and other data for financial and statutory returns * Performing reconciliations to confirm accuracy of accounts information. * Liaising with clients, fund managers and banks daily * Utilising the firm's technology solutions to manage client enquiries efficiently and effectively. **The Person** The ideal candidate will have an excellent academic record, the ability to work in a fast\-moving environment and the interpersonal skills required to interact with colleagues, clients, and international financial institutions. They should also have: * Strong aptitude for numerical accuracy and a high degree of analytical and problem\-solving ability. * 2\.1\- or first\-class honours degree ideally in a relevant discipline such a Commerce, Business Studies, Economics or Maths / Maths Related (however graduates from other disciplines wishing for a career change will also be considered if they have a strong maths aptitude) * A working knowledge of MS Excel, SQL and Data Analytics would be advantageous. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to work well within a team environment. * An ambition to develop a successful and rewarding career. * An ability to work under pressure and meet challenging deadlines. * An organised and systematic approach to their job * A strong customer service orientation * Knowledge of a European language would be advantageous but not essential. * Knowledge of Fund Accounting would be advantageous but not essential. **About FNZ** *FNZ is committed to opening up wealth so that everyone, everywhere can invest in their future on their terms. We know the foundation to do that already exists in the wealth management industry, but complexity holds firms back.* *We created wealth’s growth platform to help. We provide a global, end\-to\-end wealth management platform that integrates modern technology with business and investment operations. All in a regulated financial institution.* *We partner with the world’s leading financial institutions, with over US$2\.4 trillion in assets on platform (AoP). Together with our clients, we empower nearly 30 million people across all wealth segments to invest in their future.*

title: Junior HR Administrator
description: The Royal Marine Hotel is recruiting a Junior Human Resources Administrator. This role supports the day to day work of the HR department and helps keep things running smoothly for employees across the hotel. **This position offers up to 32 hours per week, depending on business needs.** **Role Overview** The position involves general HR administration, including maintaining employee records, assisting with hiring and supporting staff throughout their employment. The role also includes basic health and safety administration and ensuring procedures are followed correctly. **Key Responsibilities** * Keep employee files up to date, both paper and digital * Assist with job ads, reviewing applications, arranging and conducting interviews * Prepare paperwork for new starters and organise inductions * Handle leaver paperwork and update records when staff leave * Work with payroll by checking weekly hours and updating employee details * Respond to day to day staff queries and pass on issues where needed * Help organise and conduct training sessions and maintain training records * Support health and safety administration tasks * Support and conduct internal staff events, recognition activities and social initiatives across the hotel **Requirements** * Experience in an administrative role, ideally within HR or a busy office environment * Experience handling employee records, documentation, or similar administrative processes * Familiar with basic HR practices such as recruitment admin, onboarding paperwork and employee files * Comfortable using Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) and database/HR systems * Ability to manage multiple tasks at once and keep work organised without constant supervision * Strong written and verbal communication skills, including dealing with staff queries in a clear and professional way * High level of accuracy when working with records and confidential information * Previous experience in a hotel or hospitality setting would be an advantage **Benefits** * Opportunities for progression * Staff discounts across sister properties * Health club and pool membership * Discounted spa treatments and products * Complimentary golf in Druids Heath * Bike to work scheme * Free parking * Meals on shift and healthy snacks * Employee recognition programmes * Employee referral bonus

title: Junior Java Developer
description: **Graduate / Junior Java Developer** **Location:** North Dublin \- Onsite **Overview** We are seeking a motivated Graduate / Junior Java Developer to join a growing technology team based in North Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or early\-career software engineer to work on modern cloud\-based applications using Java, Spring technologies, and AWS. You will work alongside experienced developers in an agile environment, contributing to the design, development, and maintenance of scalable enterprise applications. **Responsibilities** * Develop and maintain backend applications using Java and Spring Boot * Assist in building RESTful APIs and microservices * Participate in code reviews, testing, debugging, and troubleshooting * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams including QA, DevOps, and Product teams * Work with AWS cloud technologies and CI/CD pipelines * Contribute to technical documentation and best practices * Support continuous improvement and optimisation of existing systems **Required Skills \& Experience** * Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related discipline * Strong understanding of core Java concepts * Exposure to Spring Framework / Spring Boot * Knowledge of REST APIs and web services * Understanding of relational databases such as MySQL or PostgreSQL * Familiarity with Git/version control * Good problem\-solving and communication skills * Eagerness to learn new technologies and work in a collaborative environment

title: Junior Legal Executive - Litigation
description: ***International firm with offices in Dublin 4 are expanding – these are brand new roles with lots of opportunity to grow and learn!*** Working across Dispute Resolution, Insolvency and Employment you will part of a team of two supporting a busy team. Your duties will include: * Preparing documents including briefs and booklets * Opening files * Maintaining client database * Coordinating calendars for fee\-earners * AML checks and client onboarding * Billing * Ad hoc administration This role would suit someone currently working in litigation in a small or medium sized firm. If you have experience using PractiveEvolve it would be advantageous. You will be ready for the step up to a larger, more commercial environment. In this role you will be based fully onsite at their offices just on the Dublin 2 / Dublin 4 border, this is well served by both Bus and Dart. If you have 2\+ years in a legal environment, ideally within in litigation, submit your CV for consideration today!

title: Junior M&E Quantity Surveyor - Kildare
description: We have an exciting opportunity for a Junior M\&E Quantity Surveyor to join Suir Engineering Ltd. This position is for a semiconductor project at Leixlip, Co. Kildare. Here in Suir Engineering we are a leading international provider of innovative Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation Engineering Solutions. Our core sectors are Energy, Power and Renewables, Pharma, Food \& Beverage, and Data Centres. We provide best in class contracting solutions with in\-house, flexible resources capable of delivering a personable and consistent service to our customers. Over the past 40 years we have delivered projects for Irish and multinational clients across the globe. We are currently expanding significantly, and are delivering large scale projects across Ireland, the U.K, Germany, Denmark and Sweden. We recognise that continued business success depends on our commitment to and development of our people. We are determined to create a diverse and equal workforce and to ensure our company appeals to the best and brightest people. We wish for all employees to realise their full potential and have the opportunity to grow and develop during their career with Suir. We are also working continuously to promote a culture of positive wellbeing through our wellbeing programmes and supports to employees and their families through our Employee Assistance Programme and the Lighthouse Club. **What you will be doing?** * Management and ownership of the commercial and contractual requirements through the life cycle of the project. * Responsibility for interface Client Commercial/Contract Team. * Project Procurement including Sub\-contractors. * Conduct cost and risk analysis. * Review all incoming correspondence from Employers, Design Team and /or subcontractors, to identify contractually critical items and to initiate replies. * Manage commercial aspects of subcontractors. * Provide project costing, margin \& cashflow reports. * Manage project change order procedures and agree costs with client. **Is this the job for me?** * Suitable Candidates must have a 3rd level qualification in Construction Economics / Quantity Surveying and 2\-3 years’ experience working as a Mechanical or Electrical Quantity Surveyor * Excellent oral and written communication skills. * Previous experience working in the electrical/mechanical field * Ability to prioritize workload according to deadlines together with a high attention to detail. * Ability to influence colleagues at all levels. * Enjoy working as part of a high performing team. **In Return From Suir Engineering** * 23 days annual leave * Company Pension contributions, Life and Disability cover * Private Health Insurance contribution * Educational Assistance, CPD, Chartership supports and upskilling opportunities * Opportunities for international assignments to work on one of our European projects in Sweden, Denmark, or Germany. Our way of working is called the Suir Way, designed to help us on a journey of continuous improvement. * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. *Suir Engineering are an equal opportunities employer, we value our greatest asset …. our People.*

title: Junior Marketing Assistant
description: Part\-Time Marketing Assistant **KBB Digital – Dublin / Hybrid** About KBB Digital KBB Digital is a specialist digital marketing agency serving the kitchen, bedroom, bathroom, joinery, and construction industries. We work with premium brands and independent businesses to help grow their online presence through social media marketing, content creation, photography, videography, website development, and AI\-powered marketing solutions. Due to continued growth, we are looking for a creative and motivated **Part\-Time Marketing Assistant** to join our team. Role Overview This is an exciting opportunity for someone with a passion for digital marketing, social media, and content creation. The successful candidate will support the management of existing customer accounts while also helping grow KBB Digital’s client base. The role involves creating engaging social media content, assisting with photography and videography projects, supporting website updates, and leveraging platforms such as Meta, LinkedIn, and AI\-based marketing tools to drive results for clients. Key ResponsibilitiesSocial Media Management * Manage and schedule content across Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn * Create and publish a mix of posts, reels, stories, and promotional content * Maintain consistent branding and engagement across multiple client accounts * Monitor social media performance and identify opportunities for growth * Assist in developing creative content strategies for clients Content Creation * Support photography and videography shoots for client projects * Edit and repurpose content for social media and web use * Organise and manage existing client content libraries * Create marketing assets using Canva, Adobe tools, and AI\-assisted platforms Website \& Digital Support * Assist with ongoing website updates and portfolio uploads * Support content population for new website projects * Work alongside the team on AI\-supported website development initiatives Business Development Support * Assist in identifying and onboarding new customers * Support marketing campaigns aimed at growing KBB Digital’s customer base * Help prepare marketing materials and proposals **Ideal Candidate** We are looking for someone who is: * Creative, organised, and self\-motivated * Passionate about social media and digital marketing * Comfortable working across multiple projects and brands * Interested in interiors, design, construction, or creative industries * Keen to learn and use emerging AI marketing tools Skills \& ExperienceEssential * Experience managing social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn) * Strong content creation skills * Good understanding of Meta Business Suite and LinkedIn * Basic photography/video editing experience * Excellent communication and organisational skills Desirable * Experience with Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, or CapCut * Knowledge of WordPress or website content management * Understanding of AI marketing tools and automation platforms * Experience running paid social campaigns Role Details * Part\-time position * Flexible/hybrid working options available * Opportunity to grow with an expanding agency * Work with leading brands in the KBB and construction sectors How to Apply Please send your CV, portfolio/examples of social media work, and a short introduction outlining your experience and interest in the role. Pay: €15\.00\-€20\.00 per hour Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Junior Quantity Surveyor, Dublin, Ireland Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including: Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Review and understanding project contract documentation. * Preparing and analysing supplier and sub\-contractor enquiries. * Preparation and issuing of contract documents to our supply chain. * Monitoring change in our scope of work and pricing of variations. * Preparing and agreeing Monthly progress applications. * Financial and contractual control of sub\-contractor accounts. * Issuing of contractual notices and evaluate loss and expense/acceleration claims. * Providing fast, accurate, competitive and profitable estimates. * Preparation of monthly commercial project reports. * Provide Data and analysis of issues \& risks as required by Management. * Managing and forecasting of project Cash flows. * Liaising with the client, main contractor and other departments. * All other duties associated with this role. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Knowledge of COMM’s and ICT scope of Work and Systems * Academically (Degree) qualified and or relevant experience in the industry * 5\+ years’ experience in a QS role or engineering role with good commercial exposure * A wide understanding of subcontracts * We are looking for candidates from CSA and MEP discipline areas. * Able to negotiate with clients and subcontractors on a professional level * Experience in use of Excel a requirement (SAP experience an advantage) * Excellent Analytical and problem\-solving skills * High level of accuracy and attention to detail * Must have fluency in written and spoken English for this QS Job in the UK ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** **.** Määrälaskija (ICT), Suomi Mercury on Euroopan johtava rakennusratkaisujen toimittaja. Rakennamme ja hallinnoimme monimutkaisia suunnittelu\- ja rakennusprojekteja maailman johtaville yrityksille. Ratkaisumme auttavat tarjoamaan teknologioita ja elämää mullistavia edistysaskeleita, jotka yhdistävät ihmisiä, yhteisöjä ja yrityksiä ja antavat heille mahdollisuuden saavuttaa uskomattomia asioita. Ihmisillämme olla rohkeutta olla innovatiivisia. Heidän päättäväisyytensä ja tarkka keskittymisensä antavat meille mahdollisuuden toimittaa varmuudella, aika aika jälkeen. Toteutamme asiakkaidemme visioita huippuluokan rakennusratkaisuilla useilla eri aloilla, mukaan lukien: yritysten datakeskukset, edistynyt teknologia ja biotieteet, hyperskaalatut datakeskukset ja palontorjunta. Mercurylla velvollisuutemme on kannustaa ja tukea ihmisiä toteuttamaan visionsa itsestään. paikka heidät toimintamme keskiöön ja tarjoamme haastavia kehitysmahdollisuuksia loistavassa tiimissä tukevassa ympäristössä, joka antaa heille mahdollisuuden saavuttaa täyden potentiaalinsa. **Roolin Keskeiset Vastuut** * Tarkista ja ymmärrä projektisopimusdokumentaatio. * Toimittajien ja alihankkijoiden tiedustelujen valmistelu ja analysointi. * Sopimusasiakirjojen valmistelu ja toimittaminen toimitusketjullemme. * Työn laajuuden ja variaatioiden hinnoittelun muutosten seuranta. * Kuukausittaisten edistymishakemusten valmistelu ja hyväksyminen. * Alihankkijoiden tilien taloudellinen ja sopimusvalvonta. * Sopimusilmoitusten antaminen sekä tappio\- ja kulu\-/kiihtyneiden maksujen korvausvaatimusten arviointi. * Tarjoamme nopeita, tarkkoja, kilpailukykyisiä ja kannattavia kustannusarvioita. * Kuukausittaisten kaupallisten projektiraporttien laatiminen. * Tarjoa johdon edellyttämiä tietoja ja analyysejä ongelmista ja riskeistä. * Projektin kassavirtojen hallinta ja ennustaminen. * Yhteydenpito asiakkaan, pääurakoitsijan ja muiden osastojen kanssa. * Kaikki muut tähän rooliin liittyvät tehtävät. **Rooliin Liittyvät Keskeiset Kriteerit** * Tietoa viestinnän ja ICT:n työtehtävien ja järjestelmien laajuudesta * Akateeminen (tutkinto) pätevyys ja/tai asiaankuuluva kokemus teollisuus * Vähintään 5 vuoden kokemus laadunvalvonnan tai insinöörin tehtävissä ja hyvä kaupallinen kokemus * Laaja ymmärrys alihankinnoista * Etsimme ehdokkaita CSA\- ja MEP\-tieteenaloilta. * Kykyä neuvotella ammattimaisesti asiakkaiden ja alihankkijoiden kanssa * Kokemus Excelin käytöstä on välttämätöntä (SAP\-kokemus katsotaan eduksi) * Erinomaiset analyyttiset ja ongelmanratkaisutaidot * Korkea tarkkuus ja yksityiskohta huomioiminen * Tässä QS\-työpaikassa Isossa\-Britanniassa sinulla olla sujuva englanti sekä kirjallisesti että suullisesti. ***Mercury on tasa\-arvoinen työnantaja*** **.** Apply Now

title: Junior Recruitment Consultant: Fast-Track Your Career
description: Junior Recruitment Consultant: Fast\-Track Your Career * **Five Promotions in Two Years, Not the Exception, the Expectation** * **Progress Measured by Merit, Not by Time Served** * **Your Career Journey, Finally Under Your Control** You’ve mastered managing clients, meeting deadlines, and delivering exceptional results under pressure in the events sector. Now it’s time to use those skills to build a career in recruitment that moves as fast as you do. In events, recognition can take years, and sometimes it depends more on who notices than what you achieve. At Archer, your progress is entirely in your hands. Hit clear, achievable targets, and watch your career and financial earnings soar. Every milestone is mapped, every promotion earned, every success rewarded. **Why Join the Team?** * **Fast\-Track Your Career:** Use your client and people skills in recruitment to accelerate your rise. Your results drive promotions, not office politics, with the potential for up to 5 promotions in just 2 years, faster than in events. * **Be Recognised \& Rewarded:** Every deal you close and every relationship you build translates directly into career growth and financial reward. Promotions are earned based on performance, giving you real recognition for your effort. * **Take Control of Your Future:** Recruitment lets you shape your own path. Move from executing plans to driving results, influencing decisions, and building authority, all while creating the financial freedom and lifestyle you’ve been aiming for. **What You Will Do:** * **Client Management:** Build and maintain strong relationships with clients from a varied range of industries. * **Candidate Sourcing:** Proactively source, screen, and qualify candidates for various roles within the IT sector. Utilise a variety of sourcing methods, including LinkedIn, networking, and database management. * **Market Insight:** Stay informed about the IT industry, including trends, challenges, and opportunities. Use this knowledge to inform recruitment strategies and provide insights to clients and candidates. **What You Will Have:** * 3\+ years of commercial experience \- 1–3 years of experience in the events industry (Event Planner, Event Coordinator, Event Manager, PR \& Events Consultant, etc.) * A results\-driven mindset and hunger to succeed * Strong people skills, communication, and resilience **What You’ll Gain** * **Unlimited commission** with no caps * **Clear promotion roadmap** with visible milestones * **Monthly and quarterly incentives** for standout performance * **1\-to\-1 mentorship** from top performers who’ve done the journey themselves **Total Compensation for an Average Earner:** * **Year 1 – €48,000\+** * **Year 2 – €71,000\+** * **Year 2 – €100,000\+** * An uncapped salary structure means top performers earn way above this! **Ready to Turn Effort into Career Growth?** Contact Ciara O’Flynn at **ciara.oflynn@archer.ie** or **\+353 1 902 6804** to start your journey with **clear milestones, rapid progression, and a path to leadership.**

title: Junior Site Engineer (Civil)
description: NOLCIV Engineering is looking to onboard a full time Junior Site Engineer / Graduate Engineer with minimum 0\-2 years experience for projects based across the South East of Ireland. Wicklow/Wexford/Carlow/Waterford/Kilkenny. **Key Responsibilities** * Daily Setting out, infrastructure, and site works in line with architectural and engineering drawings. * Assist the Site Manager with daily construction activities and site coordination. * Carry out quality checks and maintain accurate records throughout the project. * Monitor works to ensure compliance with specifications, standards, and safety requirements. * Collaborate with subcontractors, suppliers, and the wider site team. * Report on progress, issues, and technical matters as required. * Support the development of as\-built drawings and handover documentation. * Ensure works are delivered safely, efficiently, and to a high standard of finish. **Requirements** * Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or related discipline. * Clear communication skills and ability to work as part of a busy site team. * Strong attention to detail and proactive approach to problem\-solving. **What We Offer** * On site training * Long term work * Career progression. * Company Pension * Company Vehicle \& Fuel Allowance Job Type: Full\-time **To Apply** Email your CV to info@nolciv.ie Job Types: Full\-time, Contract Contract length: 12 months Pay: €40,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Wicklow, County Wicklow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Do you hold a civil engineering, construction management or related degree? Experience: * Site Engineer: 1 year (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: Junior Technician - Transportation Planning
description: Transport Planning Europe Region DUB0000VO **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin.** Join our Transportation Planning Team to work on a large variety of impactful public and private projects undertaken by our key clients. The Transportation Planning Team works on the forefront of projects that directly face the development of sustainable communities and climate challenges. We work on high impact projects that brings global best practice, innovation, creativity and technical brilliance together. Our work builds towards Arup’s purpose which is to ‘shape a better world’ by staying committed to developing solutions that address the climate crisis, nature loss, social equity, the need to transition to more sustainable economies and to bring greater prosperity and opportunity to people’s lives. Transportation is a key economic sector impact in the creation of a resilient society and our team works at the forefront of this field to find opportunities where conventional designs can be influenced. **The Opportunity** To support our continued growth and expansion into new markets we are seeking a junior technician to join our Transportation Planning team to work collaboratively with other engineers, planners and technicians on a wide variety of projects. These projects will typically include new public realm schemes, transport infrastructure and development masterplans. The candidate will work under the leadership of a senior technician who will direct the work to be carried in a fast paced environment where the candidate will be expected to learn as work progresses. The role offers lots of opportunities to advance within the organisation both in Ireland and globally. This is an opportunity to join the Transportation Planning team on the forefront of technical design and to gain on hand experience and technical skills and knowledge alongside experts in this field. There will also be opportunity for structured training and mentorship. **As a Junior Technician at Arup, you will:** * Assist in the design and delivery of a variety of transport projects, collaborating with experienced engineers and multidisciplinary teams. Produce high\-quality detailed General Arrangement drawings, plans and sections using AutoCAD. Production of 3D models using AutoCAD Civil 3D, Revit and other BIM software * Work under instruction given by senior technician or senior engineers working within the transportation team. From time to time you might do work with other disciplines / teams within Arup. * Contribute to discussions on design options available to solve problems encountered * Assist in the development of other team members where required. * Contribute to the development of good practice and processes through involvement with the Arup skills network. * Attend and participate in, presentations and workshops. * In the long\-term, you will strive to understand the design process the engineers utilise within the team, helping the technicians explore the capabilities of various software, and developing ways in which the software can streamline the delivery of our work. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for graduates who is passionate about the promotion of and development of sustainable transportation and shaping a better world. We also value a candidate that is: * Currently pursuing or recently graduated with an NFQ Level 6 Higher Certificate or Advanced Certificate qualification in a technical qualification such as engineering, CAD, technical design or similar. * Able to demonstrate a sound technical base * Able to cooperate with others in a team environment effectively and contributes in generating ideas * An excellent communicator who brings teamwork skills and the ability to contribute effectively in a collaborative environment * Working to a plan and quality standards and meeting deadlines set * Understands how to develop self in self, in design, technical and generic skills **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits that work for you:** * **Professional Growth \& Development**: Kickstart your career with our structured graduate development program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * **Global Opportunities**: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * **Financial Well\-being \& Ownership**: Receive a competitive graduate salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing program. * **Wellbeing and Culture:** At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. \#IREGrad **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Legal Counsel
description: Legal Counsel Full Time **Permanent** position Hybrid role based in our City Centre offices, with on\-site attendance three days per week (Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday). **What we offer** We have embraced a hybrid working model for most of our positions, which means that you can enjoy a balanced approach of working from home for part of the week and working from the office for the remainder of the week. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salaries and bonuses, robust Learning and Development support, excellent Defined Contribution pension and comprehensive Wellbeing initiatives and support to name but a few. Further details on our benefits package can be accessed here Benefits (life\-careers.com) **Role Overview** We are seeking a talented and commercially minded transactions lawyer with 2 – 4 years’ post\-qualification experience to join our growing Legal team. Experience in reinsurance is not essential. Our experienced team will share their knowledge and mentor the successful candidate in the nuances of the reinsurance sector. The role will involve collaborating with our global Legal team to provide legal support on reinsurance businesses across the EMEA, US and APAC regions. You will draft and review contract wordings and, with the support of our team, participate in business engagements to understand how reinsurance transactions are negotiated, drafted, and finalised. The role offers the opportunity to work on high‑value and sophisticated reinsurance transactions, commercial projects, as well as supporting our global business on other legal matters with the support of external counsel, where necessary. **About the Team** Our team supports reinsurance business throughout the EMEA, US and APAC regions. We work collaboratively to draft, review, and negotiate reinsurance contract wordings that protect our company's interests while meeting client needs. We pride ourselves on our legal expertise, business acumen, and ability to translate complex legal concepts into practical solutions that drive our business forward. **About You** The ideal candidate will have a commercial mindset and can translate complex legal language into clear, precise, and accessible business context. A team player at the beginning of your career, you bring enthusiasm for learning and growing within the insurance/reinsurance sector. You have a collaborative approach, strong attention to detail, and solid analytical skills. Your communication abilities enable you to collaborate effectively with colleagues and progressively build confidence in client interactions. You are adaptable, eager to learn and take ownership of your development within a positive and supportive environment. **Responsibilities** * Build legal expertise in the creation and drafting of reinsurance and other agreements with support and guidance from senior colleagues. * Review and analyze reinsurance contracts and related documents to ensure alignment with business intent and corporate requirements. * Learn compliance frameworks to align contracts with applicable laws, regulations, and internal requirements. * Participate in negotiations alongside experienced team members, gaining exposure to discussions with internal partners, external counterparties, and brokers. * Contribute to innovation by supporting AI\-driven enhancements of contract management IT infrastructure and tools. * Assist in the creation of training materials and support with training delivery. * Apply governance standards to contract wordings and processes. * Work closely with internal stakeholders across Legal, Compliance, Actuarial, Business Development, Underwriting, and Executive teams. * Provide clear, pragmatic legal advice on regulatory and governance matters. **Education \& Experience:** * University degree in law. * A minimum of 2 years’ post qualification experience gained in a reputable law firm and/or in‑house legal department. * Experience in negotiating and drafting transaction agreements and other commercial contracts. **Knowledge \& Skills:** * Strong understanding of general corporate and commercial principles. * Knowledge and experience of privacy law, finance/banking law, the reinsurance, or insurance sectors or in a regulated industry is desirable, but not essential. * Attention to detail and a thorough and analytical mindset. * Strong organisational and project‑management skills, with the ability to manage multiple legal workstreams. * Proactive and solution focused approach to problem‑solving. * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent people/interpersonal and client service skills; professional and positive demeanor and experience working collaboratively as part of a team. * Agile, pragmatic, and can\-do attitude and a willingness to learn. **About us** Canada Life Reinsurance (CL Re) is a division of Great\-West Lifeco Inc. (Lifeco) and a leading provider of client focused reinsurance solutions in the United States, Europe and Asia. CL Re offers a range of innovative risk and capital management solutions covering mortality, longevity, health, lapse, investment, non\-life and P\&C risk for insurers, reinsurers, and pension funds. CL Re is continuing to expand the solutions it offers to new and existing clients in many global markets. CL Re has offices in Dublin, Toronto, Philadelphia, Bermuda, Barbados, and Regina. The Dublin office is the base for CL Re’s European and Asian operations as well as the Irish reinsurance entity, Canada Life Re Ireland dac (CLReI). It comprises approximately 180 reinsurance professionals, including 120 actuaries and trainee actuaries. It is a proactive, inspiring, and friendly environment where talent and hard work are recognised and rewarded. Lifeco is a Canadian headquarter, international financial services holding company with interests in life insurance, health insurance, retirement and investment services, asset management, and reinsurance businesses. Lifeco and its companies have approximately 33,000 employees, $3\.3 trillion in consolidated assets under administration and a capital ratio (LICAT) of 128% (as at 31/12/2025\). As a Company we are committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace where employees can thrive and reach their full potential. The company reserves the right to draw up a shortlist as part of the selection process. Where Agency assistance is required the Canada Life Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Canada Life Reinsurance by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role. Canada Life Group Services is proud to be an Equal Opportunities employer. We have created an environment of inclusion and growth to help people achieve their potential, to ensure they can bring their whole selves to work and to feel valued. We celebrate diversity of thought and perspective and want all of our employees to feel valued, respected and supported. We have built a culture that promotes equality and inclusion for all of our current and future employees and strive to ensure that our behaviours, processes and practices are aligned to this culture. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process please contact Lifecareers@irishlife.ie and we will be delighted to ensure you are fully supported to be your best. CLGS supports Equal Opportunity and is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: MES Engineer
description: **About Business Platforms:** Headquartered in Cork, Ireland, Business Platforms specialises in Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), Serialization, and Supply Chain Operations for our clients. We provide consulting services globally to the Pharmaceutical Industry in * Strategy Development and Execution * Business Process Analysis and Design * Change Management * Validation * Implementation and Support We partner with our clients to provide expertise and industry best practice to create and realize business value. **Role : MES Engineer** **Duration : Full time position** **Location : Dublin** **(Hybrid Working can be accommodated Client Site and WFH)** **Requirements of a Business Platforms MES Engineer :** * **3 \- 5 years’ experience** in GxP Manufacturing is required within the Pharmaceutical / Life Sciences Industries. * **3 \- 5 years’ experience** in MES projects involving MBR/Recipe design and qualification. * Demonstrate experience of aligning the client business process requirements with the MBR (Master Batch Records) technical design, participating in design review workshops, to ensure a successful project. * MBR/Recipe test protocol design and execution is required based on GxP regulations. * MES System Master data configuration and documentation. * Authoring and revision of applicable SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures). * Experience of training and supporting MBR/Recipe end users and improving site procedures. * Excellent interpersonal skills are required, strong verbal, digital and written communication skills * An enthusiastic, positive, and proactive attitude. * Team player, working with cross functional stakeholders, reporting to the client Project Manager (PM), ensuring milestone deliverables are met throughout. **Education :** * Hold a minimum of an Honours Level 8 degree in a Technical/Science/Engineering/ Business Information Systems discipline * Certification in Korber PAS/X or Rockwell PharmaSuite or Emerson Syncade is an advantage. **Remuneration \& Benefits :** * Business Platforms offers an extremely competitive base salary, commensurate with experience and skill\-set. * Great company benefits; medical and dental insurance, life assurance and pension. * Sports \& social events. * 23 days holidays p.a. * Business Platforms offers the opportunity for an accelerated career path with a holistic approach to lifelong learning, bespoke one\-on\-one training and support which will enable you to acquire new skills and develop. * At Business Platforms you will work as part of our Global MES Team, working with industry experts. You will be contributing to a variety of unique and challenging projects. * You can expect a positive culture, a collaborative and supportive environment where we encourage new ideas and innovation, to achieve the highest standard of outcomes for our clients.

title: Management Accountant
description: **Job Title:** Management Accountant – Client Portfolio \& Advisory **Sector:** Professional Services **Location:** South Dublin (Hybrid) **Salary:** €70,000 \+ Bonus **Our Client** Our client is a high\-growth international professional services firm with a rapidly expanding presence in Ireland. The firm provides accounting, compliance, advisory and regulatory services to a broad range of domestic and international clients and has built a strong reputation for delivering high quality client service in a collaborative and people\-focused environment. **Why should you apply?** This is an excellent opportunity for a practice\-trained accountant to join a growing Accounting \& Compliance team in a highly client\-facing Management Accountant role. This position offers the chance to step into a key leadership role within an expanding team while taking ownership of a portfolio of clients across management accounting and statutory reporting assignments. The role offers strong commercial exposure and genuine progression opportunities within a business that is continuing to grow both locally and internationally. You will gain significant client ownership, mentor junior staff and work closely with leadership on managing profitability, developing client relationships and delivering a high\-quality service offering. The firm also provides excellent hybrid flexibility alongside strong internal leadership and management development programmes. **Who should apply?** This role will suit a qualified accountant coming from practice who is currently operating at Assistant Manager or Manager level and looking to step into a broader client leadership role with greater ownership and progression opportunities. The successful candidate will likely have the following experience: * Qualified Accountant (ACA / ACCA / CPA) * Practice\-trained with experience in management accounts and statutory financial statements * Strong client relationship management and stakeholder engagement skills * Experience reviewing work and mentoring junior team members * Commercially minded with an interest in managing client portfolios and assignment profitability * Strong technical understanding of Irish financial reporting and compliance requirements **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting into the Accounting \& Compliance leadership team, this role will play a key position in supporting the continued growth of the division while managing a varied portfolio of clients. * Client Portfolio Management – Manage a portfolio of clients across management accounts and statutory financial statement assignments * Client Advisory – Build strong client relationships and advise clients on accounting and compliance matters * Financial Reporting – Oversee the preparation and review of management accounts and year\-end financial statements * Team Leadership – Mentor and review the work of junior team members as the team continues to grow * Commercial Exposure – Manage assignment budgets, billing and profitability across client engagements * Stakeholder Management – Work closely with senior leadership and clients to ensure high quality delivery across assignments * Process Improvement – Identify opportunities to improve efficiencies and enhance client service delivery * Business Development – Support the growth of existing client relationships and identify opportunities for additional services **Interested in this position?** To apply please submit your CV to Frank Bailey who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact us directly on 01 529 4200\.

title: Manufacturing Technician
description: **Manufacturing Technician** We are currently partnering with an innovative and fast\-growing medical materials company to recruit a Manufacturing Technician to join their expanding operations team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or early\-career professional looking to build their experience within a highly regulated biotech, pharmaceutical, or medical device manufacturing environment. The successful candidate will support the manufacture and supply of medical\-grade biopolymer products within a cGMP and ISO 13485 cleanroom environment **Key Responsibilities** * Manufacturing and processing of products in line with SOPs * Packaging, labelling, and dispatch support * Maintaining production records and documentation * Supporting quality and compliance activities * Cleaning and upkeep of laboratory and production equipment * Assisting with equipment calibration and maintenance activities * Working closely with manufacturing, quality, and process engineering teams **Required:** * Qualification (Level 7 or above) in Science, Engineering, Manufacturing, Biotechnology, or related discipline * Previous experience in a manufacturing, laboratory, pharma, biotech, or medical device environment is advantageous * Experience working in GMP or cleanroom environments is desirable * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Hands\-on and proactive approach to work * Strong team player with good communication skills This is a fantastic opportunity to join a collaborative and growing company offering excellent exposure within a cutting\-edge manufacturing environment.

title: Marketing Coordinator
description: *If you are a current employee who is interested in applying to this position, please navigate to the internal Careers site to apply.* **About MarketStar** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. **About The Marketing Coordinator** We are looking for a B2B marketing and operations professional who understands both how messaging is built and how it performs in the field. This is not a pure content role — it is a hybrid of marketing operations, sales enablement, and regional adaptation, where you will take globally developed messaging frameworks and translate them into market\-relevant, high\-performing sales plays for EMEA teams. If you are someone who enjoys working at the intersection of strategy, execution, and field impact, this role will be a strong fit. **Location:** Hybrid, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 (Tuesday, Wednesday \& Thursday in office) **What will you do?** * Adapt and localize globally approved email templates and sales cadences to align with EMEA\-specific industries, personas, and market nuances. * Serve as the primary liaison between central strategy teams and EMEA sales teams, ensuring alignment with regional priorities. * Embed sales plays into rep workflows through structured rollout, enablement sessions, and field engagement. * Align outreach with regional campaigns, pipeline priorities, and buyer context. * Capture and synthesize feedback from sales teams to identify adoption gaps and messaging improvements. * Monitor adoption trends and execution quality across regions, surfacing insights to central teams. * Ensure consistency between global frameworks and local execution while maintaining design principles and guardrails. * Create content that aligns with the buyer journey, motivating action across digital platforms. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to align messaging with strategic priorities. * Conduct in\-depth research to deliver industry\-specific narratives and insights. * Track content performance and refine strategies based on key metrics **Skills \& Qualifications** * Proven experience in creating B2B marketing content across campaigns, with exposure to SaaS or tech industries being a plus. * Experience in marketing operations, or sales enablement, preferably in SaaS or enterprise technology environments. * Strong understanding of email campaigns, sequencing, and demand generation workflows. * Experience working with CRM and sales engagement platforms (e.g., Salesforce, Salesloft). * Ability to adapt messaging for multi\-geo campaigns, with sensitivity to regional nuances. * Strong collaboration skills with experience working across sales, marketing, and operations teams. * Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret performance signals and translate them into actionable insights **What we offer?** Through commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Company Pension Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Education reimbursement * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is basic salary per annum of €85000 per annum. There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process.* *MarketStar is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual who identifies as someone with a disability and require reasonable accommodation(s) to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at* *people.success@marketstar.com* *for assistance.*

title: Marketing – Graduate Programme
description: **Description** Ryanair Labs is the technology wing of Ryanair, Europe’s Largest Airline Group. Based in our state\-of\-the\-art digital and IT innovation hub, Labs is focused on creating Europe’s leading travel experience for millions of customers across the Ryanair network. Our Labs teams consist of software development, BI \& data, cybersecurity, infrastructure, QA, project planning \& delivery and GEN AI professionals. As a Marketing Graduate, you will help bring this work to life by showcasing the people, projects and innovation happening across Ryanair Labs. **The Role** We are delighted to announce that applications are now open for our 2026 Marketing Graduate Programme for Ryanair Labs. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative, organised and ambitious marketing graduate to join a fast\-paced technology environment and support the promotion of Ryanair Labs across internal and external channels. While you will collaborate with different marketing and internal teams, this will be a standalone position giving you ownership of the marketing strategy. Key Responsibilities * As a Marketing Graduate, you will support the Ryanair Labs team across a wide range of marketing and communications activities, including: * Creating engaging marketing content to promote Ryanair Labs, our people, projects and technology teams * Producing short\-form video content for social media, careers campaigns, events and internal communications * Filming and editing videos using tools such as CapCut and Canva * Supporting the planning and delivery of social media campaigns * Assisting with copywriting for social posts, newsletters, blogs, careers pages and internal updates * Helping to develop employer branding content that showcases life at Ryanair Labs * Capturing photo and video content at Labs events, graduate and team initiatives * Working with stakeholders to gather stories, interviews, project updates and content ideas * Bringing fresh ideas to help improve how Ryanair Labs communicates its culture and technology work * Assisting with event marketing, including careers fairs, university events, internal showcases and technology meetups. **Requirements** * Graduated with a minimum 2\.1 degree in Marketing or a related discipline * Graduated/finished university no earlier than 2024 * Experience creating short\-form video content for social media platforms * Video filming and editing skills, ideally using tools such as CapCut and Canva. * Strong copywriting skills with the ability to adapt tone of voice for different audiences * Good visual eye for content, layout, branding and storytelling * Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines * Confidence working with different teams and stakeholders to gather content and ideas * Ability to work on your own initiative and bring new ideas to the table * Strong interpersonal and communication skills * Good PC skills, including Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint * A positive attitude, willingness to learn and a good sense of humour — doesn’t take themselves too seriously! **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies Adaptability/Flexibility Creativity/Innovation Dependability Initiative Communication Interpersonal Skills

title: Mechanical Engineer - Ireland
description: At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission\-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting\-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good. Your impact Ireland’s water sector is undergoing a major transformation. Unprecedented investment and technological innovation are creating a once\-in\-a\-generation opportunity to redefine water and wastewater infrastructure. At Jacobs, we’re proud to be at the forefront of this transformation—partnering with Uisce Éireann (Irish Water), Northern Ireland Water, and the Department for Infrastructure (DfI) to deliver critical water and wastewater services, flood management solutions, and environmental protection projects. These initiatives make a real difference to communities across Ireland. We’ve secured multiple long\-term frameworks and programmes, giving our teams the chance to work on projects that span the entire lifecycle—from strategic planning and feasibility studies to detailed design, construction, and commissioning. We’re looking for Mechanical Engineers at all levels—from ambitious early\-career professionals to experienced leaders—to join our teams across Ireland. Whether you’re starting out or ready to take on a senior role, you’ll work on detailed design and Design \& Build projects for water, wastewater, and bioresources infrastructure. You’ll design and deliver mechanical systems for pumping stations, treatment plants, and associated assets, ensuring compliance with regulatory and safety standards. Depending on your experience, you’ll contribute to or lead engineering activities throughout the project lifecycle—collaborating with multidisciplinary teams and clients to achieve outstanding results. Key responsibilities * Deliver Mechanical Design Excellence Design water and wastewater systems, including pumping stations, treatment plants, and associated infrastructure, from concept through completion. * Innovate \& Add Value Participate in value engineering and buildability workshops, delivering sustainable and innovative design solutions. * Project Lifecycle Involvement Develop feasibility studies, outline and detailed designs, and provide construction and commissioning support. * Technical Documentation Produce design layouts, technical specifications, datasheets, and P\&IDs for mechanical systems. * Equipment Selection Specify mechanical equipment such as pumps, blowers, screens, valves, chemical dosing plant, and odour control systems. * Site Engagement Conduct site surveys and investigations, liaising with clients, contractors, and suppliers. * Compliance \& Standards Ensure designs meet Irish and UK water industry standards, health \& safety, and environmental regulations. * Team Development Mentor junior engineers and contribute to building a strong, collaborative team culture. * Collaborative Delivery Work closely with civil, electrical, and process engineers to deliver integrated solutions. * Project Management Support or lead design management, scheduling, risk management, and financial forecasting. Here's what you'll need * Working toward or holding chartered status (e.g., IMechE or Engineers Ireland). * Experience ranging from developing core mechanical engineering skills to 12\+ years in detailed design and Design \& Build execution for water, wastewater, and bioresources projects. * Strong understanding of mechanical engineering principles, project lifecycle management, and mechanical equipment used in water/wastewater treatment. * Familiarity with Irish water sector regulations, health \& safety, and environmental standards. * Excellent communication, collaboration, and organisational skills; leadership experience for senior roles. * Robust understanding of health, safety, and welfare legislation. * Experience with design tools (e.g., AutoCAD, Revit, BIM) is advantageous. Why Join Jacobs? * Grow Your Career – From early talent to seasoned professionals, we offer clear paths for progression and development. * Shape the Future of Water – Work on innovative, high\-impact projects that protect resources and drive sustainability. * Collaborate with the Best – Partner with industry\-leading clients and experts in a thriving, future\-focused sector. **Ready to make a difference?** Join Jacobs and help deliver smarter, sustainable water solutions for generations to come. Apply today and be part of the change! Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees. With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well\-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs. We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here. Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles. As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role. We value collaboration and believe that in\-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work. Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.
