title: Met us at NIBRT? Start your career in Medical Information with ProPharma
description: For the past 20 years, ProPharma has improved the health and wellness of patients by providing advice and expertise that empowers biotech, med device, and pharmaceutical organizations of all sizes to confidently advance scientific breakthroughs and introduce new therapies. ProPharma partners with its clients through an advise\-build\-operate model across the complete product lifecycle. With deep domain expertise in regulatory sciences, clinical research solutions, quality \& compliance, pharmacovigilance, medical information, and R\&D technology, ProPharma offers an end\-to\-end suite of fully customizable consulting solutions that de\-risk and accelerate our partners’ most high\-profile drug and device programs. Did we meet you at the National Institute for Bioprocessing Research and Training careers event? Are you a recent (or soon\-to\-be) graduate in Life Sciences, Nursing, Pharmacy, or Medicine with a strong interest in patient safety and the pharmaceutical industry? If you're curious about where your degree could take you next, we’d love to stay connected. **Why consider a career in Medical Information?** Medical Information (MI) is a unique entry point into the pharmaceutical and biotech industry. It sits at the heart of patient safety, scientific communication, and real\-world healthcare impact. **At ProPharma, Our Medical Information Teams** * Provide accurate, evidence\-based information on medicines and devices * Support healthcare professionals, patients, and the public * Play a key role in drug safety and regulatory compliance * Work in a fast\-paced, collaborative, and global environment It’s a role where you can apply your scientific knowledge from day one—while continuing to learn and grow. What could your future role look like? **As a Multilingual Medical Information Specialist (MLMIS), You Would** * Respond to medical and safety enquiries via phone, email, and digital platforms * Communicate complex scientific information in a clear, professional way * Support pharmacovigilance activities (e.g. adverse event reporting) * Work with international teams and global pharmaceutical clients * Develop expertise in therapeutic areas and medical communication We regularly look for graduates with fluency in English plus another European language (e.g. French, German, Spanish, Italian, Dutch). **What We Look For In Graduates** * A degree in a Life Science or healthcare\-related discipline * Strong communication skills and attention to detail * Interest in patient safety, medical communication, or the pharma industry * A proactive, curious mindset and willingness to learn * Language skills (depending on role requirements) **Why join ProPharma?** * Structured graduate\-friendly onboarding with full training provided * Clear career progression – many of our leaders started in entry\-level MI roles * Flexible, remote working opportunities * Exposure to global pharmaceutical clients and projects * A supportive, collaborative, and multicultural environment **Stay connected with us** Even if you're not ready to apply just yet, we’d love to hear from you. Register your interest to be considered for upcoming Multilingual Medical Information Specialist (MLMIS) opportunities. Simply submit your CV and we’ll keep you in mind for future roles that match your profile. We celebrate our differences and strive to create a workplace where each person can be their authentic self. We are committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. Employees are encouraged to unleash their innovative, collaborative, and entrepreneurial spirits. With a holistic approach as an Equal Opportunity Employer, we provide a safe space where all employees feel empowered to succeed. All applications to roles at ProPharma are personally reviewed by a member of our recruitment team. We do not rely on AI screening tools to support our hiring process. You will always receive an outcome to your application so that you have an answer from us \- whether you're successful or not. Whilst ProPharma supports remote working, we also recognise the value that comes from in person collaboration. As such, we encourage any new hires that are based within a reasonably short commute of one of our offices to work on a hybrid basis and spend some time working from that office location, as agreed with your manager. All applications will be treated on their own merit and candidates will not be at any advantage or disadvantage based on their proximity to an office. \*\*\*ProPharma Group does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters/third parties. Please, no phone calls or emails to anyone regarding this posting.\*\*\*

This is an excellent opportunity for a reliable and skilled tradesperson to join a busy and professional workshop environment. Key Responsibilities * Fabrication and assembly of metal components from technical drawings * Cutting, grinding, welding, and finishing materials to specification * Performing quality checks to ensure high standards are maintained * Assisting with general workshop duties as required Requirements * Previous experience in metal fabrication or a similar role * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings * Strong attention to detail and ability to work independently * Good timekeeping and reliability * Safe Pass / manual handling certification Position Details * **Contract Length:** 2 months * **Start Date:** Immediate start available * **Location:** Bohernabreena, Dublin 24 and on site RCT preferable but can also be PAYE Pay: €25\.00\-€30\.00 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer
description: **Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer** **Full Time Permanent Role** **Citywest Office (Hybrid) or UK (Remote)** **The Role** Uniphar are embarking on an ambitious, multi\-year transformation program and are seeking a Dynamics 365 CE Developer to support our teams across our transformation initiatives. We are seeking an experienced and dynamic developer to join our team. The ideal candidate will possess a strong background in Dynamics 365 Customer Service. The Dynamics 365 Developer will play a crucial role in supporting \& enhancing existing functionality along with building out greenfield Dynamics 365 Customer Services solution as we continue to grow and innovate. **Key Responsibilities** Technical Experience * Configuring Dynamics 365 CE solutions, specifically in Customer Service environments. * Operate effectively within a Managed D365 environment * Design, build and maintain Power Automate flows to streamline and automate business processes. * Deploy solutions using CI/CD pipelines (manual deployment to test \& production environments will not be allowed) * Manage and maintain Git\-based source control, ensuring proper versioning and collaboration practices * Support the CRM Architect by contributing to documentation, configuration oversight, and integrations. Dynamics CRM Experience * Configure CRM components including but not limited to Tables, Views, Forms, Business Process Flows, Security Roles, and Workflows. * Ability to develop plugins and JavaScript libraries * Support development of and maintaining Canvas Apps where needed. * Maintain up\-to\-date skills on new features and solutions. Project Responsibilities: * Working within the guidelines and to the objectives defined by any project or program currently running. * Working within an Agile sprint structure * Prioritise work tasks and report updates and status reports to any relevant project streams. **Requirements** * 5\+ years Dynamics 365 development experience, preferably with Customer Engagement (CE/CRM) * Strong knowledge of Dynamics 365 Customer Service \& Power Platform * Ability to design innovative, re\-usable solutions and components. * Experience of building plugins, workflows, Power Automate flows, and Logic and Canvas apps. * Experience of designing integrations in Azure * Experience with ALM tools (e.g., Git for Enterprise, Azure Devops). * Strong troubleshooting skills in a cloud and solutions architecture context. * Ability to collaborate effectively with cross\-functional teams. * Proven ability to analyse complex technical issues and implement solutions based on industry best practices * Experience of building web apps in Azure or SAP integration is an advantage * Good working experience with SaaS solutions and integrations. **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialised services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process. *Please note that Uniphar is an equal\-opportunity employer; we do not discriminate and welcome all responses.* \#uniphargroup \#unipharjobs

title: Microsoft Enterprise Architect
description: **Enterprise Architect** * Location: Dublin * Function: Information Technology * Level: Senior / People Manager **The Opportunity** * Our client is a large, globally operating organisation headquartered in Dublin is seeking an experienced **Microsoft focused Enterprise Architect** to join its senior IT leadership team. * The organisation has recently completed a major technology transformation and is now focused on optimisation, architectural maturity, and the adoption of next‑generation technologies including AI. * This is a senior people‑management role, reporting at executive IT level, with responsibility for defining enterprise architecture direction, governing complex solutions, and shaping long term technology strategy across a modern application, data, and cloud environment. **Core Capability Focus** The successful candidate must have strong consolidation skills and people management experience. This is a **Microsoft first** role, with deep emphasis on the **Azure ecosystem** rather than multi‑cloud generalism. **AWS to Azure migration** would be a huge benefit. This person should be focused on native **Azure AI capabilities** , not Copilot. **The Role** The Enterprise Architect will operate at the intersection of business strategy, enterprise architecture, and technical design, with a strong emphasis on: * Microsoft cloud services * Application and data integration * Analytics and modern data platforms * Pragmatic use of AI to drive measurable business value **Experience \& Qualifications** * Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent professional experience * Extensive experience in Enterprise and Solution Architecture roles * Strong hands‑on and governance experience on Microsoft Azure and Microsoft platforms * Proven use of Microsoft Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) and Azure Well‑Architected Framework (WAF) * Deep experience designing modern data and analytics platforms * Practical experience applying AI services in an enterprise environment with appropriate controls * Proven people management experience and senior stakeholder engagement capability * Ability to influence both technical and non‑technical audiences at executive level **Key Responsibilities** Architecture Delivery * Design and govern enterprise and solution architectures primarily on the Microsoft platform * Set architectural direction across application, data, analytics, and cloud landscapes * Architect modern data and analytics platforms leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Azure * Define and govern data integration and orchestration patterns using Azure/Fabric Data Factory * Ensure secure integration with third‑party platforms via APIs and approved connectivity patterns * Embed AI‑enabled capabilities using Microsoft AI services where they deliver clear business outcomes * Drive adoption of Power Platform solutions (Power Apps \& Power Automate) within architectural guardrails Architecture Governance \& Leadership * Own and evolve enterprise architecture standards, principles, and reference architectures * Lead, manage, and develop a team of Solution and Domain Architects * Chair and operate architecture governance forums and decision‑making processes * Assure that solutions are secure, scalable, resilient, compliant, and cost‑effective * Partner with senior programme and business leaders on planning, prioritisation, and delivery * Ensure compliance with organisational, regulatory, security, and audit requirements *(Please apply with your CV in Word (preferred) for PDF format. More details are available on successful application.)* If you are interested in applying for this role, please do so via the relevant link here. title: Microsoft Security Engineer
description: ### **Description** We are seeking a highly skilled and results\-driven Microsoft Security Engineer to join our technical delivery team. The ideal candidate will be a hands\-on subject matter expert in Microsoft's security ecosystem, with a strong focus on implementing, configuring, and supporting cloud security and data governance solutions within Azure, Entra, the Microsoft Defender suite, and Microsoft Purview. This role is primarily focused on technical execution—turning architectural designs into secure, compliant, and operational customer environments### **Key Responsibilities** **Key Responsibilities** **Implementation \& Configuration:** * **Deploy, configure, and maintain** core Microsoft security technologies, including **Azure Networking Security** components (Azure Firewall, NSGs, Azure WAF) and **Entra ID** services (Conditional Access, PIM, Entra Private Access). * Execute the **onboarding and fine\-tuning** of the **Microsoft Defender suite** (e.g., Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Cloud, Defender for Office 365\) across customer environments. * Implement and manage **Microsoft Sentinel** for security information and event management (SIEM), including connector deployment, playbook automation, and custom rule creation. **Data Governance \& Compliance:** * **Implement and configure Microsoft Purview** capabilities, including **Data Loss Prevention (DLP)** policies, **Information Protection** (sensitivity labels, encryption), and records management features. * Configure **eDiscovery, communication compliance, and audit log** settings within Purview to meet regulatory and data protection requirements (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001\). * Define and apply **data classification schemes** and **retention policies** across various data sources using Purview tools. **Endpoint \& Identity Management:** * Implement and manage **Microsoft Intune** policies for endpoint security, compliance, and device configuration. * Configure advanced **Identity and Access Management (IAM)** solutions within **Entra ID**, focusing on least privilege principles and identity governance. **Operational Support \& Documentation:** * Perform security **hardening** and ensure operational compliance through the implementation of Azure Policy, and resource locks. * Produce clear, detailed, and up\-to\-date **technical runbooks, configuration guides, and "as\-built" documentation** for all implemented security and compliance solutions. ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** **Technical Execution Expertise:** * Deep, hands\-on experience with the deployment and configuration of Microsoft Azure security services (Azure Policy, Network Security, Key Vault) and E5 Security. * Expert proficiency in configuring and managing Entra ID (formerly Azure AD), including advanced features like Conditional Access Policies, PIM, MFA, and SSO integrations. * Proven implementation experience with the Microsoft Defender Suite and Microsoft Sentinel. * Practical hands\-on experience implementing Microsoft Purview, specifically configuring DLP policies, sensitivity labeling, and retention labels. * Strong practical knowledge of managing security controls for Windows and mobile endpoints using Microsoft Intune. **Soft Skills \& Qualifications:** * Excellent ability to follow detailed technical implementation plans and execute tasks efficiently. * Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills to diagnose and resolve complex technical security and compliance issues. * Meticulous attention to detail in technical configuration and "as\-built" documentation. **Desirable Attributes** * Current **Microsoft certifications** such as **AZ\-500 (Azure Security Engineer Associate)**, **SC\-200 (Microsoft Security Operations Analyst)**, or **SC\-400 (Microsoft Information Protection Administrator)**. * Practical experience applying security and compliance frameworks such as **ISO 27001** or **NIST** during implementation. * Familiarity with automation and scripting tools (e.g., PowerShell, Azure CLI, ARM/Bicep templates) to streamline deployments. ### **About Codec** Established over 40 years ago, Codec is a multi\-award\-winning Microsoft Solutions Partner, trusted by public and private sector organisations to deliver impactful digital transformation. With offices in **Dublin**, **Belfast**, **Cologne**, and across **Germany**, our team of over **300 experts** specialises in harnessing the full potential of Microsoft technologies—including **Dynamics 365**, **Power Platform**, **Azure**, and **Copilot**—to drive innovation, automation, and operational excellence. Codec combines deep industry expertise with a proven track record to help organisations unlock the power of AI and modern cloud solutions Though originally best known as a hardware reseller, since 2010 Codec has focused on building an outstanding software consulting business with a team of experts implementing enterprise solutions using products from Microsoft and Oracle. Our customer base, revenues and average contract values have grown steadily year on year and we continue to invest in the provision of new product and service offerings. Our team is a careful mix of recent graduates and experienced professionals from a variety of diverse cultural backgrounds. We are a melting pot of seasoned consultants supported by the best talent from the local universities. We have a thriving placement student academy and graduate recruitment programme which grows year on year. This company is an equal opportunities employer

title: Mid-Level Data & BI Engineer – Career progression!
description: **Mid\-Level Data \& BI Engineer – Career progression!** * Data engineering \+ BI delivery * Cloud\-based pipelines \& analytics ownership * Strong growth into senior Are you a Data/BI Engineer who enjoys solving real\-world data problems, building scalable pipelines, and turning complex datasets into clear, actionable insights? A growing data\-driven organisation is looking for a Mid\-Level Data \& BI Engineer to join their team and take ownership of key data pipelines, modelling, and reporting capabilities across a modern cloud\-based data platform. This is a hands\-on role where you’ll work across data engineering and BI delivery, designing and optimising ETL/ELT pipelines, improving data models, and building high\-quality Power BI dashboards that directly support business decision\-making. You’ll have the opportunity to make a real impact within a strong senior team while continuously upskilling and progressing your career. You’ll also take on mentoring responsibilities with junior engineers, helping shape their growth. In addition, you’ll contribute to architectural discussions, drive automation initiatives, and support ongoing improvements across the data platform. **Your Mission** * Build and optimise scalable ETL/ELT pipelines in a cloud environment * Design robust data models and improve data quality and performance * Deliver insightful Power BI dashboards and reporting solutions * Troubleshoot and resolve data issues across multiple systems * Support junior engineers through mentoring and code reviews **What You’ll Bring** * \+ 5 years of experience in Data Engineering or BI development * Advanced SQL and experience working with large datasets * Hands\-on experience with data modelling and data warehousing * Cloud experience (AWS or Azure) * Python for data processing/automation **Salary \& Benefits** Competitive salary DOE \+ Bonus \+ Pension \+ Health Insurance \+ career growth opportunities For a confidential discussion, contact Olga Torres at \+353 1 649 8519 or send your CV to olga.torres@archer.ie.

title: Motor Mechanic Apprentice/ Fitter
description: Mechanical fitter or 2nd/3rd year apprentice Role working in a busy workshop, some experience is essential. Ideal candidate must have experience in some aspects of vehicle servicing and be interested/capable of producing quality work and advancing their knowledge ; this role may also suit apprentice.... We carry out all aspects in house; from servicing to extensive engine work, tyre changing experience and electric/hybrid vehicle experience a plus, attention to detail is crutial. Used to working in an environment where quality, reliability, efficiency and self sufficiency are of upmost importance. Timed served in a Motor Vehicle Apprenticeship or Modern Apprenticeship essential ; and relevant NVQ Level 3, or National Certificate in Motor Vehicle Engineering, a plus! Salary subject to experience Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Supplemental pay types: * Tips Experience: * Mechanic experience: 3 years minimum * Diagnosis: 3 years (preferred) * Salary negotiable dependent on experience and work standard. Work Location: One location Job Type: Full\-time Pay: From €27,000\.00 per year Experience: * Diagnosis: 2 years (preferred) Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Multi-Tradesman – Property Maintenance & Development | Mullingar
description: **Multi\-Tradesman – Property Maintenance \& Development** We are looking for a reliable, hands\-on **Multi\-Tradesman** to join our growing construction and property maintenance company. The role involves a mixture of property maintenance, renovations, refurbishment work, and assisting on development projects across residential and commercial properties. This is an ideal role for someone who is practical, takes pride in their work, can turn their hand to multiple trades, and enjoys variety in their day\-to\-day work. **Responsibilities** * Carry out general property maintenance and repair work * Perform multi\-trade tasks including: **Carpentry, Painting \& decorating, Basic plumbing, Tiling, Flooring, Patch plastering, Silicone/sealing work, General handyman duties** * Assist with renovation and development projects * Diagnose maintenance issues and provide practical solutions * Complete snagging lists to a high standard * Work independently or alongside other trades on site * Keep sites clean, organised, and safe * Communicate professionally with tenants, suppliers, and team members * Ensure jobs are completed efficiently and to a high standard * Help source materials and manage basic site logistics where required **Requirements** * Experience working across multiple trades * Full clean driving licence * Strong problem\-solving ability and common sense * Ability to work independently and manage workload * Good attention to detail * Reliable, punctual, and hardworking * Positive attitude and willingness to help across different projects * Own tools preferred but not essential **Desirable Skills** * Experience in small construction projects or property refurbishments * Basic electrical or plumbing knowledge * Machine/ticket experience an advantage * Safe Pass / Manual Handling certification **What We Offer** * Variety of work across maintenance and development projects * Opportunity to grow with the company * Stable, long\-term position * Supportive team environment * Overtime opportunities available * Company vehicle may be provided for the right candidate **Ideal Candidate** The ideal person is someone who enjoys practical work, can think on their feet, and takes ownership of getting jobs done properly. We value attitude, reliability, and work ethic just as much as qualifications. **Location**: Mullingar, County Westmeath **Job Type**: Full\-Time / Permanent **Salary**: Competitive salary based on experience Work Location: In person

title: New Business Sales Account Executive
description: **We help the world run better** At SAP, we enable you to bring out your best. Our company culture is focused on collaboration and a shared passion to help the world run better. How? We focus every day on building the foundation for tomorrow and creating a workplace that embraces differences, values flexibility, and is aligned to our purpose\-driven and future\-focused work. We offer a highly collaborative, caring team environment with a strong focus on learning and development, recognition for your individual contributions, and a variety of benefit options for you to choose from. SAP is looking for a New Business Sales Executive. If you are result\-driven and creative, if success as a team is a key driver, if you love the frenzy that comes with victories, if you are looking for a multicultural work environment that rewards excellence and over\-achievement, if you have a good business acumen and the ability to articulate value to business executives then feel free to apply. **What is a New business Sales Executive ?** The New Business Sales Executive is an account executive responsible for developing incremental revenue within their assigned territory in the new business segment. Fully responsible for their territory coverage, demand generation, forecasting, closing, planning \& strategy, and customer/partner satisfaction, they work closely with the different stakeholders to bring business value and innovation to its customers and prospects. **What are you looking for?** * To join a market leader in business digital transformation through technology * To join a company providing a solid career path and focus on people development * To develop your business executive and partner network **What does a New business Sales Executive day look like?** **Demand Generation** * Proactively engage with customers and partners to understand their needs and position our solution portfolio. * Demand generation planning \& execution to address territory plan and ensure proper coverage in collaboration with key stakeholders * Qualify leads, progress through sales cycle. Leverage Partner Determination Matrix and engage with key stakeholders for partner selection, offer \& pricing support to partner. **Pipeline Management** * Manage and close deals to meet or exceed monthly, quarterly and yearly targets along with partners. * Use our Digital Sales Motion to effectively engage with customers and partners. * Work with Presales, Solution Sales, Industry Experts, Channel Partners and other key stakeholders on deal execution as needed * Drive opportunity management of volume \& value opportunities. Focus on insuring customer needs are understood and addressed in the sales process. * Maintaining accuracy of CRM data for pipeline management and customer intelligence **Self\-Development** * Focus Area – Develop deep solution \& process understanding in designated solution areas * On top of on\-the job coaching as provided by Sales Leadership, the AE should improve their sales skills along various dimensions as defined in the SAP Sales Career progression framework. Aes are expected to actively work with their management to use the framework to build sales and other skills. **What you bring:** * Minimum 2\-3 years’ experience in New business Sales environment/Field sales, respectively Demand Generation * Minimum 1\-2 years´ experience as Quota Carrier (Mandatory) * Successful experience in multi\-channel go to market models * Knowledge of the ERP market would be beneficial **Skills you’ll use:** **Professional Skills** Customer Orientation Effective Communication Establishing Trust Collaboration Results Orientation **Tech Industry \& SAP General Skills** Software as a Service (SaaS) Artificial Intelligence Process Improvement Technology Innovation SAP Cloud Suite Portfolio RISE and GROW with SAP SAP Corporate Strategy **Role Specific Skills** Cloud Strategy Complex Sales Customer Engagement Customer Relationship Management Industry Knowledge Negotiation Sales Forecasting Sales Qualification Competitive Positioning Account Governance **Bring out your best** SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end\-to\-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose\-driven and future\-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best. **We win with inclusion** SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well\-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better and more equitable world. SAP is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provide accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e\-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training. **EOE AA M/F/Vet/Disability:** Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, age, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability. Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor. Requisition ID: 430833 \| Work Area: Sales \| Expected Travel: 0 \- 10% \| Career Status: Professional \| Employment Type: Regular Full Time \| Additional Locations: \#LI\-Hybrid.

title: Office & Facilities Operations Executive
description: *If you are a current employee who is interested in applying to this position, please navigate to the internal Careers site to apply.* **About MarketStar** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. We are excited to have you apply to join our MarketStar team and can’t wait to discuss how we can help you find growth! **About The Office \& Facilities Operations Executive** The Office \& Facilities Operations Executive is a hands\-on, individual contributor role responsible for the day\-to\-day running of the Dublin office. The role is primarily focused on facilities and workplace operations (approximately 85%), with a smaller element of administrative and executive support (approximately 15%). This role suits someone with prior office or facilities experience who is comfortable working independently, coordinating vendors and stakeholders, and keeping the workplace running smoothly, safely, and professionally. **Role \& Responsibilities** Facilities \& Workplace Operations (85%) * Manage the day\-to\-day operation of the Dublin office, ensuring a safe, professional, and well\-maintained working environment. * Coordinate cleaning, maintenance, building management systems (BMS), workspace setup, and general office upkeep, including monitoring stock levels of office supplies and consumables. * Act as the main operational point of contact for landlords, building management, and external service providers. * Coordinate vendor activities, manage service providers, track service levels (SLAs), and support facilities\-related procurement. * Track facilities\-related budgets, invoices, and routine operational spend, escalating variances where needed. * Support and coordinate Health \& Safety activities, including maintaining documentation, supporting risk assessments, coordinating incident reporting, and escalating issues in line with company policies and statutory requirements. * Act as a facilities contact during building incidents or emergencies (e.g. fire alarms, building issues), coordinating with building management and internal stakeholders. * Serve as a trained First Aider and Fire Warden (training will be provided) and support ongoing workplace safety and wellbeing initiatives. * Coordinate IT and workplace equipment needs with internal IT teams, including workstation setup and basic asset tracking. * Resolve day\-to\-day facilities or operational issues promptly, escalating more complex matters where appropriate. * Manage visitor access in line with Facilities SOPs (pre\-visit, check\-in, post\-visit follow\-up) and coordinate catering when required. * Work closely with the Information Security team to support physical security controls, including identifying and escalating physical security issues. * Provide regular operational updates to the Facilities Leadership team, covering completed and upcoming works, vendor performance, incidents, and spend. Administrative \& Executive Support (15%) * Support new\-hire onboarding, including workspace setup, access badges, induction coordination, and equipment preparation, in line with internal data protection policies. * Respond to employee queries related to office and facilities matters. * Provide ad\-hoc administrative support to executives, such as meeting coordination, travel bookings, and calendar assistance, where business needs require. * Support office culture and engagement activities, including events and team initiatives. * Maintain facilities and office records, trackers, and documentation to support smooth operations and audit readiness. * Assist with occasional executive schedule changes to accommodate client meetings. **Skills \& Qualifications** * Previous experience in office management, facilities coordination, or workplace operations. * Proven experience coordinating vendors and day\-to\-day facilities services. * Good working knowledge of workplace Health \& Safety practices in Ireland; experience supporting compliance activities in a regulated environment is desirable. * Strong organisational and time\-management skills, with a practical, hands\-on approach. * Comfortable working independently and managing competing priorities. * Clear communicator with the ability to handle sensitive information discreetly. * Experience supporting onboarding and office logistics. * Fluent in spoken and written English. **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Company Pension Plan * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Education reimbursement * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is basic salary per annum of €40,000 \- €45,000* . *There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process.* *MarketStar is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual with a disability and require reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at* people.success@marketstar.com *for assistance.*

title: Operations & Outsourcing Co-Ordinator (12 month Fixed Term Contract)
description: Fineco Asset Management DAC (‘Fineco AM’) is a Dublin based Asset Management Company, and asubsidiary of FinecoBank S.p.A. (“FinecoBank”). FinecoBank (“Fineco”) is a leading European bank with a 20\-year history and a fully digital DNA with a branchless approach since the start. Fineco is one of the banks with the widest products range available. Fineco have developed a truly one\-stop\-solution which includes trading, investment and payment services. Fineco have developed a 100% proprietary platform for trading and investment which covers all customer needs from trading listed and OTC product to investment product such as mutual funds and ETF. Fineco AM, which started its operations in May 2018, is authorized by the Central Bank of Ireland as a UCITS \& AIFM Management Company with a MIFID top up, and it currently has approximately €39bn in assets under management.Fineco AM is a competence centre\- specialised in the management of fund\-of\-funds, single strategy funds, as well as Structured Solutions Funds. In addition to portfolio management activities, all key supporting and controlling functions, such as Compliance, Risk, Finance and Legal, are also performed locally. The company started with 5 employees and has experienced strong and consistent growth, with a current headcount of approximately 90 FTE’s. Currently Fineco AM manages 3 UCITS Umbrella Funds – 1 in Luxembourg and 2 in Ireland **Main Responsibilities:** We are looking to recruit enthusiastic professionals, reporting to the Head of Operations to support our UCITS Management Company business. * Be the first point of contact with the investment managers, in relation to outstanding issues as they arise, including settlement, invoicing of the funds and cash flows; * Be the first point of contact with custodian and the fund administrator, in relation to outstanding matters regarding settlement and dividend distribution and any other corporate actions; * Liaise with custodian(s) to open new accounts on behalf of sub\-funds, sub\-Advisors, etc. * Liaise with tax advisors to ensure proper and timely WHT filing/CGT rate applied and rebates reclaimed. * Support overall delegate/outsourced service oversight programme in accordance with company’s internal policies and procedures * Manage the end\-to\-end operational set\-up requirements for static data, notifications, filings, etc. for new fund launches * Collect, review and validate fund expense fees including administration, custody, other fund service providers whilst ensuring they are in line with our funds’ General Expense Budgets; manage communications with investment managers and service providers to ensure timely and accurate payment * Contribute to the production and maintenance of a General Expenses budget for the funds and review, on a periodic basis accruals in the funds NAVs against invoiced amounts. * Assist in the periodic review of NAVs, EMIR Reporting. Ongoing Charges etc. as produced by the Firm’s delegates. Report, track to resolution any exceptions observed. **Key Requirements:** * Third level qualification in Accountancy, Finance or a Business discipline. * 2\-3 years' experience in Operations/Middle Office/Custody. * Good knowledge of financial products and financial markets. * Very good knowledge of Excel. * Working knowledge of AI tools an advantage. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Strong attention to detail. * Well organized with strong time management skills. * Good team player. * Be enthusiastic and eager to learn, self\-starter. **What we offer:** * **Growth Opportunities:** Unlock your potential with career growth and professional development paths. * **Innovative Team:** Be part of a dynamic and innovative team environment. * **Collaborative Culture:** Thrive in a collaborative company culture that values your contributions. * **Generous Time Off:** Enjoy 25 days of holidays to relax and recharge. * **Hybrid Working:** Benefit from a flexible hybrid working policy, 3 days in the office, 2 days flexibility to be remote. * **Health and Dental:** Comprehensive Healthcare and Dental coverage for your well\-being.

title: Operations Risk & Resilience Placement - 2026 Work Placement
description: Assets and Operations Europe Region DUB0000S1 **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin and Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. The Operations Consulting team supports clients across the full asset lifecycle—from acquisition and planning through to operation and disposal—by addressing the interdependencies between people, processes, systems, and assets. The discipline delivers a broad and integrated set of services, including: * **Operational Strategy \& Transformation:** Helping clients align operational execution with strategic goals, including digital transformation initiatives and process improvement * **Governance, Risk \& Compliance (GRC):** Support the delivery of effective compliance and risk management programmes to identify, manage and mitigate business and digital related risk * **Operational Readiness \& Performance Improvement:** Ensuring systems, teams, and processes are prepared for launch or transition, and continuously optimised for performance * **Digital Enablement:** Leveraging tools like BIM, Digital Twins, and analytics platforms to improve operational visibility and decision\-making **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Cork or Dublin team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. You will be working with a team of Operations Consulting Services professionals from various offices. The Operations Consulting team embodies Arup’s Total Design philosophy by integrating people, processes, technology, and physical assets into cohesive operational strategies that are embedded from concept through to implementation. The team works with a variety of public sector organisations, including Transport Infrastructure Ireland. With reference to our Risk \& Resilience service within Operations Consulting, it exemplifies Arup’s Total Design by embedding resilient governance frameworks, proactive risk intelligence, and compliance assurance into the operational phase of projects—ensuring that systems, processes, and people are holistically integrated. This is an office\-based role, with successful candidates expected to work 5 days per week in the Arup office in Cork or Dublin. **As a work placement student in the Operations team at Arup, you will:** * Support turning technical recommendations into operational plans and project deliverables with defined sustainability outcomes for our clients * Practice creative applications of technology to develop design solutions * Develop communication skills, including report writing and visual representations * Attend and participate in meetings – listen, ask questions * Be involved in discussions with team members to gain knowledge \& experience * Undertake analytical research to develop, document, present concepts and design ideas * Review project documentation, applicable codes and standards to assist with preparation of reports, presentations, drawings and/or sketches * Give structure to ideas – lots of “taking a first pass” at spreadsheets, reports and analyses for discussion later rather than instruction **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for students who are currently studying for a relevant bachelor’s degree level or be currently undertaking a post\-graduate level qualification in a relevant discipline, including, but not limited to: * Governance \& Compliance Management * Business in Operations Management * Management (Operations \& Supply Chain) * Supply Chain Operations * Business Information Systems * Computer Systems Engineering * Cybersecurity We also value: * Experience of producing technical presentations and reports, knowledge sharing with peers * History of academic research, technical presentations, and technical report writing * Experience with any of the following: GRC software solutions, Cybersecurity solutions, Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, or Project, or analysis packages * Knowledge of ISO Assurance Frameworks such as 27001 or 55001 and Data Privacy regulations * Evidence of teamwork and leadership in academic or extracurricular field * The ability to prioritise effectively, balance assigned work and deliver to deadlines **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits that work for you:** * **Professional Growth \& Development**: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * **Wellbeing and Culture:** At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern \#LI\-Onsite **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Payroll Administrator II
description: **About Pinterest** Millions of people around the world come to our platform to find creative ideas, dream about new possibilities and plan for memories that will last a lifetime. At Pinterest, we’re on a mission to bring everyone the inspiration to create a life they love, and that starts with the people behind the product. Discover a career where you ignite innovation for millions, transform passion into growth opportunities, celebrate each other’s unique experiences and embrace the flexibility to do your best work. Creating a career you love? It’s Possible. At Pinterest, AI isn't just a feature, it's a powerful partner that augments our creativity and amplifies our impact, and we’re looking for candidates who are excited to be a part of that. To get a complete picture of your experience and abilities, we’ll explore your foundational skills and how you collaborate with AI. Through our interview process, what matters most is that you can always explain your approach, showing us not just what you know, but how you think. You can read more about our AI interview philosophy and how we use AI in our recruiting process here. We’re looking for an experienced **Payroll Administrator** to oversee the day to day operations of the International Payroll function. In this key role, you’ll ensure timely, accurate payroll processing for all non US countries and help drive business initiatives to support our expanding global workforce. Join a collaborative, fast paced team committed to operational excellence and exceptional employee support. **What You’ll Do** * Manage end to end payroll processing for assigned countries in partnership with external providers, including preparation, review, and reconciliation of payroll inputs and reports each pay period to ensure accuracy and compliance. * Develop and maintain payroll calendars, checklists and standard operating procedures across all assigned entities. * Deliver monthly and annual payroll statutory obligations, ensuring timely and accurate payroll tax remittances, social security contributions, and other required filings for assigned countries. * Act as the primary point of contact for payroll inquiries, resolving employee questions in a timely and professional manner. * Serve as the primary point of contact for payroll discrepancies, ensuring clear, timely communication and resolution updates for all stakeholders. * Partner cross functionally on business initiatives and payroll projects, particularly with US based teams, including Equity, Benefits, Employment Law, Global Mobility, and HR. * Support special projects and ad hoc initiatives, including expansion, compliance, and operational efficiency efforts as the company continues to scale internationally. * Partner with internal stakeholders and external vendors to resolve payroll related issues and ensure compliance with local regulations. * Maintain up to date expertise on international payroll laws, regulations, and industry best practices. **What We’re Looking For** * 3\+ years of international payroll operations experience processing payroll in multiple countries, preferably across EMEA and APAC or LATAM. * Experience working with global payroll platforms and third party vendors in a fast growing, high volume payroll environment. * Strong understanding of global payroll practices, tax and reporting regulations, and compliance requirements. * Resourcefulness and strong problem solving skills to resolve complex payroll issues quickly. * Exceptional organizational skills, ability to juggle multiple responsibilities with various deadlines and deliver accurate results. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills to partner effectively with teams across functions and geographies. * Payroll certifications (CIPP, IPASS, APA, or equivalent) and Workday experience are a plus. * Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field or equivalent experience. **Relocation Statement:** * This position is not eligible for relocation assistance. Visit our PinFlex page to learn more about our working model. **In\-Office Requirement Statement** * We recognize that the ideal environment for work is situational and may differ across departments. What this looks like day\-to\-day can vary based on the needs of each organization or role. * This role will need to be in the office for in\-person collaboration 2 times per week and therefore needs to be in a commutable distance from the Dublin office. **Our Commitment To Inclusion** Pinterest is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We want to have the best qualified people in every job. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion or religious creed, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, status as a protected veteran, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information or characteristics (or those of a family member) or any other consideration made unlawful by applicable federal, state or local laws. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. If you require a medical or religious accommodation during the job application process, please complete this form for support.

title: Payroll Professional - Europe
description: At BBH, Partnership is more than a form of ownership—it’s our approach to business and relationships. We know that supporting your professional and personal goals is the best way to help our clients and advance our business. We take that responsibility seriously. With a 200\-year legacy and a shared passion for what’s next, this is the right place to build a fulfilling career. HR Professional \- Payroll Europe. Location: DUBLIN Brown Brothers Harriman Global Payroll team is currently recruiting a Payroll HR Professional to join our EU Payroll Team. This role is responsible for managing payroll activities and working directly with our out\-sourced payroll provider to timely and accurately pay our workforce across multiple key locations. This is including but not restricted to, Poland, Luxembourg, Ireland (Dublin), United Kingdom (London), Switzerland (Zurich). This position processes and verifies the accuracy of all payment processing, as well as tax filings. The leader hired for this role will also assist in researching assessments received from third party vendors and verifying items have been processed accurately in the payroll including garnishments. In addition, this person will be expected to identify and drive continuous improvement opportunities across payroll and some aspects of general HR operations. **Some Of Your Key Responsibilities Include** Payroll Processing and Verification: * Calculate and verify payroll information for both salaried and hourly BBH (permanent, temporary and intern) employees in Krakow, Dublin, London, Luxembourg and Zurich * Preparation of draft reports for the correct processing of payroll inputs * Prepare standard withholdings and deductions from gross pay amounts. Audit paychecks, benefits adjustments, special bonus awards and commissions and ensure they are accurately processed * Complete Quarterly and Annual payroll tasks Balance payroll records and approve tax payments, as required * Collaborate with appointed third\-party vendor to ensure accurate payroll for all BBH employees Payroll Reconciliation \& Reporting: * Perform payroll reconciliation, including pension payments * Resolve monthly payroll errors Complete regulatory reports as needed * Conduct analyses and prepare reports as required * Perform month end reporting procedures to ensure compliance with internal and external procedures and regulations * Support year\-end reconciliation processes **Other** * Identify and drive continuous improvements in payroll * Help educate employee base on their payroll, provide employee support as needed * Support key payroll initiatives **Qualifications** * Experience processing payroll in any or all BBH EU locations (experience with Poland, Zurich, London, Luxembourg and Dublin) ) * Experience coaching and leading others, and guiding junior team members * Experience with both in\-house and outsourced payroll models a plus * Exceptional organization, planning, and communication skills \- detail oriented * Exceptional analytical skills and ability to identify and drive continuous process improvements * Ability to manage multiple, high priority tasks and adjust to shifting priorities, while meeting deadlines * Ability to work both independently and collaborate in a team environment * Experience with payroll and HR systems especially Workday HCM * Knowledge of relevant payroll taxes and legislation a plus * Knowledge of London \& Dublin pension rules a plus * Ability to handle highly sensitive data with confidentiality and integrity * Excellent PC skills, including Microsoft Office Suite and systems interfaces * Experience in the financial services industry is desirable but not required * Fluent in English \& Polish * Minimum bachelor’s degree is a mandatory **We value diverse experiences.** We value diverse experiences and transferrable skillsets. If your career hasn’t followed a traditional path, includes alternative experiences, or doesn’t meet every qualification or skill listed in the job description, please do go ahead and apply. **About BBH** Brown Brothers Harriman (BBH) is a premier global financial services firm, known for premium service, specialist expertise, technology solutions and partnership approach to client management. Across Investor Services and Capital Partners, we work with an enviable roster of sophisticated clients who make BBH their first call when they are tackling their hardest challenges. Delivering for our clients and each other energizes us. We believe that how we do our work is just as important as what we do. We are relentless problem solvers who know our best ideas come from collective debate and development—so we are never possessive about our ideas. Every day we come together as a diverse community of smart and caring people to deliver exceptional service and expert advice—creating success that lasts. No matter where you sit in the organization, everyone is empowered to contribute their ideas. BBHers can pick up the phone and call any colleague, and they are happy to help. Expanding your impact beyond your daily role is part of how we operate as trusted partners to one another. We believe stability is a competitive advantage, but being stable means having the knowledge, skill, and discipline to evolve, often—pushing the boundaries of innovation. As a private partnership, every investment we make is in the relationships, technologies, products and development we believe are in the long\-term interests of our clients and our people. Our long\-tenured leaders are experts in their areas and are actively involved in the day\-to day business, taking the time to provide guidance and mentoring to build the next generation of BBHers. Because we know, our success begins with yours. Go to BBH.com to learn more about our rewards and benefits, philanthropy, approach to sustainability or how we support you to thrive personally, physically and financially.

description: We currently have an exciting opportunity for a **Pharmacy Technician** to join the team in our **McCauley Fairgreen Pharmacy.** This is a **32****\-hour permanent** position with the store's opening hours operating between **9 a.m. to 6p.m. Monday to Wednesday, 9a.m. to 8p.m. Thursday to Friday, 9a.m. to 7p.m. on Saturday and 12 p.m. to 6p.m.on Sunday.** The team currently comprises a **Retail Store Manager, Assistant Store Manager, Supervising Pharmacist, 1 Support, 1 Relief, and 5 Technicians.** This store has **manageable items and services.** **We can offer you:** * Competitive Hourly rates * Great Colleague Discount * Bike to work scheme * Uniform provided **The responsibilities of the role** * Be accurate with excellent attention to detail. * To prepare and dispense medicinal products under the supervision of the Pharmacist in a caring and professional manner. * Controlling stock levels and ordering medicines as determined by store requirements. * Preparation of blister packs and processing and tracking claims. * Reporting to and supporting the Pharmacist in a busy dispensary. **Requirements:** * Experience working in a busy dispensary is an advantage, but newly qualified technicians are welcome to apply. * Experience with McLernons MPS (an advantage). * IPU Pharmacy Technician course or Higher Certificate in Pharmacy Technician Studies an advantage ***If you would be interested in being considered for current or future vacancies within a dispensary role, please apply or declare your interest to our Talent Acquisition Specialist Danielle Gillen at DGillen@uniphar.ie***

title: Power Platform Functional Consultant

integrations. * Contribute to the design of **Power Pages** and external facing experiences. * Support adoption of **Copilot experiences** and AI assisted features where appropriate. * Collaborate closely with architects and developers to ensure best practice solutions. * Support testing, user acceptance, training, and golive activities. * Act as a trusted advisor to customers on Power Platform capabilities and roadmap. **Core Skills \& Experience (Essential)** * 3\+ years’ experience working with Microsoft Power Platform and/or Dynamics 365\. * Strong experience with model driven apps and Dataverse. * Proven ability to lead functional design and customer conversations. * Experience mapping business processes to technical solutions . * Good understanding of Power Platform security, environments, and ALM concepts . * Basic programming skills (ability to write and understand simple code). * Excellent communication, documentation, and stakeholder management skills. * A pragmatic, delivery focused mindset with a strong customer orientation. **Advantageous / Nice to Have** These skills are not mandatory, but would be considered a strong advantage and enable deeper collaboration across delivery teams: * Basic to intermediate experience with C\# or .NET (e.g. understanding plugins or extensibility patterns). * Familiarity with JavaScript for form scripting or light customization. * Exposure to custom connectors, plugins, or PCF controls. * Experience with Power Platform ALM, Git, or Azure DevOps pipelines. * Understanding of Azure services such as Azure Functions, Logic Apps, or Service Bus. * Experience supporting or collaborating on more complex enterprise integrations. **What We Offer** * Opportunity to work on modern, enterprise grade Power Platform solutions. * Exposure to the latest Microsoft technologies, including Copilot and AI capabilities. * Clear progression path across functional, architectural, or hybrid roles. * Support for learning, certifications, and professional development. * Collaborative, people\-first culture with a strong focus on quality delivery. **About Us** Spanish Point Technologies is a Microsoft focused consultancy delivering endtoend digital solutions across Power Platform, Dynamics 365, Azure, and Microsoft 365\. We partner closely with customers to remove complexity, increase productivity, and deliver measurable business value. **Application Process:** Please submit your application and CV through our online application portal. Send your application to recruitment@spanishpoint.ie. For any inquiries about the position or questions regarding the application process, you can contact recruitment@spanishpoint.ie. Join our team to contribute to the development of cutting\-edge solutions and advance your skills in the dynamic field of modern web development using Microsoft technologies. For the most current information about the position, please contact recruitment@spanishpoint.ie*

description: **Headcount Solutions** are seeking to recruit a Process (Automation) Engineer for a clients pharmaceutical site in Dublin. **Role** Based in Dublin, the role will involve close collaboration with clients and cross\-functional project teams throughout Ireland and Europe. **Role Overview** The successful candidate will contribute to the delivery of high\-quality automation and control system solutions for complex, fast\-paced pharmaceutical projects. The role includes leading technical activities, supporting project execution, and contributing to the continued growth of the engineering project portfolio. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead the development of functional requirements within an S88 framework. * Provide technical guidance and mentoring to junior and intermediate engineers. * Ensure all design activities comply with project specifications, industry standards, and regulatory requirements. * Participate in client meetings and workshops to define and develop project scope. * Build and maintain effective working relationships with clients, contractors, and internal project teams. * Prepare detailed design packages and construction documentation for process automation contractors and system integrators. * Support commissioning activities, FAT/SAT testing, and technical issue resolution during project execution and construction phases. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Bachelor’s Degree in an Engineering discipline. * Approximately 8 years’ experience in Instrumentation \& Controls engineering. * Strong experience delivering projects within the pharmaceutical or biopharmaceutical sector. * Proven expertise in process automation systems for pharmaceutical manufacturing environments. * Familiarity with GxP environments, batch process structures, equipment modules, unit phasing, process systems, and flow path design. * Excellent communication, coordination, and problem\-solving abilities. * Ability to work independently while contributing effectively within a collaborative team environment.

title: Process Engineer ( 12 Months Agency Contract )
description: Rottapharm Limited Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high\-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life's moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. Our global portfolio includes best\-in\-class, iconic brand\-name products as well as global key brands; generics, including branded and complex generics; and other offerings across a wide range of therapeutic areas. We are committed to helping create healthier communities worldwide through education, outreach and better access to treatment. For those driven to live their lives with purpose, Viatris helps employees realise their full potential by creating a collaborative culture that fosters courage and resilience. Viatris is more than just a place to work. This is a place to make a difference in the world. Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the Process Engineer role will make an impact: Key responsibilities for this role include: * Leading and Managing projects for the introduction of new products, equipment and processes to meet all safety, quality, regulatory and operational requirements. * Process mapping, gap analysis/identification for new and existing pharmaceutical manufacturing processes. * Carrying out investigations into manufacturing processes and deviations. * Coordinating changes using the Viatris approved change control procedure to ensure that cGMP is adhered to. * Project management and co\-ordination including planning, scheduling, task management and delegation, milestone planning, communication and reporting. * Managing and executing process and equipment trials and validation. * Supporting validation activities for project activities, including review of validation documentation and attendance at validation activities (FAT, IQOQ, etc.). * Ensuring that all new equipment is bought in accordance with the EEM (Early Equipment Management) policy. * Working across teams as part of project planning and deployment, including collaboration with supporting departments, such as Production/Operations, Quality, Safety, Regulatory Affairs, Facilities and Engineering, Supply Chain and Finance, etc. * Troubleshooting and optimisation of existing processes to achieve product output targets. * Generation, review and approval of project documentation (Scope, User Requirement Specifications etc), trial reports and Standard Operating Procedures. The minimum qualifications for this role are: * BEng in Chemical and Processing Engineering or appropriate science or engineering discipline. * A minimum of 3\-4 years’ recent experience in a process engineering role or similar in the Pharma/Biotech/Chemical Industry. * Understanding the Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Process and experience with dry blending, compression, coating and/or granulation (wet and dry). * A proven track record in process development, support and improvement is desirable. * The ability to organise, plan and execute multiple tasks to tight schedules. * Strong leadership skills and the ability to work in cross\-functional team environments, as well as independently. At Viatris, we are dedicated to building a truly diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t fully align with every requirement, we still encourage you to apply. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles. **Diversity \& Inclusion at Viatris** At Viatris, diversity and inclusion are essential to our mission. The diversity we foster in all aspects of our business can be one of our greatest strengths in redefining healthcare not as it is, but as it should be. If you would like to know more about what diversity, equity and inclusion means to us, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/Careers/Diversity\-and\-Inclusion **Sustainability at Viatris** Corporate social responsibility is fundamental to the Viatris mission. We work to advance responsible and sustainable operations and leverage our collective expertise to empower people to live healthier at every stage of life, recognizing that our actions affect the stakeholders and communities we serve. To learn more about our efforts, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/about\-us/corporate\-responsibility At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Process Technician Apprentice
description: **Process Technician Apprenticeship** ***Are you looking to kick start a career as a Process Technician and get an education while getting paid?!*** If so, Pfizer Grange Castle has an apprenticeship opportunity within a state\-of\-the\-art Biopharmaceutical manufacturing plant for you! *In partnership with the Irish MedTech Association \& IBEC our Engineering network within Pfizer Grange Castle we are seeking candidates for a Process* *Technician Apprenticeship.* *This is a 2\-year program combining part time learning and work placement, leading to attainment of Higher Cert (Level 6\) in Bio Process and Chemical Process Technician.* This programme includes both technical and academic content with a key focus on manufacturing. This is an opportunity to join a world class facility who is dedicated to bringing therapies that will significantly improve patients’ lives. We are looking for an **Apprentice Process Technician** to join our team, this role will support manufacturing operations by assisting with routine production activities, including equipment preparation, material handling, in‑process checks, basic equipment verification. The role includes supporting sampling activities, preparation of materials or solutions for manufacturing use, stock control, and maintenance of production equipment and tools. This role is an on the job, off the job learning. **You will be required to attend college for 15 weeks block release at TU Dublin** **You will return to Pfizer for 9 months. When onsite in Pfizer Grange Castle, you will work on a Shift Pattern which may include Days \& Nights. This will be discussed in advance!** **About the Role and Responsibilities:** You will be directly involved in the manufacturing of our products, ensuring that they meet all specifications for safety, potency, and efficacy. You will work within the guidelines of current Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) to ensure conformance with all regulatory requirements and site Standard Operation Procedures (SOP’s), while assisting in the improvement of all current processes through our Continuous Improvement Program. * Assist in maintaining a safety orientated culture, cGMP compliant work environment always. * Perform initial troubleshooting of issues identified during routing operations. * Assist with the investigation of operational deviations through Quality System, engaging with relevant functions as appropriate. * Provide input into the creation and maintenance of area SOPs and batch records. * Preparation of materials for batches and batch manufacture of bio pharmaceutical products. * Use Lean tools as part of daily operations (e.g., Method 1, 5S) to optimise efficiency and drive culture of Continuous Improvement and Zero Defects. * Assist in facility start up, installation and validation activities to support the introduction of new products * This is a shift\-based role (For 24/7 Operations), Day and Night shifts. **Candidate requirements:** * Minimum Leaving Certificate. * Have a passion for Science and/or Technology, Engineering. * Demonstrate a strong work ethic. * Work well as part of a team. * Self\-motivated / self\-starter * Excellence in systems and practical applications. * Have good time management \& prioritisation skills * Be committed to excellence in operational and compliance requirements * Focused on process improvements within a structured environment. * Willingness to learn and grow. * Willing to do Shift Work. * Willing to start September 2026 **Additional Information** * In order to be considered for this position in Ireland you need to be legally eligible to work in Ireland. * Please note there is no relocation support available for this position **How To Apply** * Make a difference today, all suitable candidates should apply with CV below. We are looking forward to hearing from you! Purpose **Breakthroughs that change patients' lives** ... At Pfizer we are a patient centric company, guided by our four values: courage, joy, equity and excellence. Our breakthrough culture lends itself to our dedication to transforming millions of lives. Digital Transformation Strategy One bold way we are achieving our purpose is through our company wide digital transformation strategy. We are leading the way in adopting new data, modelling and automated solutions to further digitize and accelerate drug discovery and development with the aim of enhancing health outcomes and the patient experience. Equal Employment Opportunity We believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial to building a successful business. As an employer, Pfizer is committed to celebrating this, in all its forms – allowing for us to be as diverse as the patients and communities we serve. Together, we continue to build a culture that encourages, supports and empowers our employees. Disability Inclusion Our mission is unleashing the power of all our people and we are proud to be a disability inclusive employer, ensuring equal employment opportunities for all candidates. We encourage you to put your best self forward with the knowledge and trust that we will make any reasonable adjustments to support your application and future career. Your journey with Pfizer starts here! Support Services
